  1047	        private static bool VerifyP256RawSignature(byte[] publicKeyBytes, byte[] signatureRaw, byte[] messageDigest)
  1048	        {
  1049	            // Upstream noble p256.verify accepts r||s of length 64. Anything
  1050	            // else is an invalid signature; return false.
  1051	            if (signatureRaw.Length != 64)
  1052	            {
  1053	                return false;
  1054	            }
  1055	            try
  1056	            {
  1057	                var curve = ECNamedCurveTable.GetByName(CryptoConstants.CURVE_NAME);
  1058	                var domainParams = new ECDomainParameters(curve.Curve, curve.G, curve.N, curve.H, curve.GetSeed());
  1059	
  1060	                var point = curve.Curve.DecodePoint(publicKeyBytes);
  1061	                var publicKeyParams = new ECPublicKeyParameters(point, domainParams);
  1062	
  1063	                var r = new BigInteger(1, signatureRaw, 0, 32);
  1064	                var s = new BigInteger(1, signatureRaw, 32, 32);
  1065	                byte[] derSignature = new DerSequence(new DerInteger(r), new DerInteger(s)).GetDerEncoded();
  1066	
  1067	                var signer = SignerUtilities.GetSigner("NONEwithECDSA");
  1068	                signer.Init(false, publicKeyParams);
  1069	                signer.BlockUpdate(messageDigest, 0, messageDigest.Length);
  1070	                return signer.VerifySignature(derSignature);
  1071	            }
  1072	            catch
  1073	            {
  1074	                return false;
  1075	            }
  1076	        }

  1083	        /// match signer key from bundle").

  1085	        private static bool VerifyEnclaveSignature(
  1086	            string? enclaveQuorumPublic,
  1087	            string? signatureHex,
  1088	            string? signedDataHex,
  1089	            string? dangerouslyOverrideSignerPublicKey)
  1090	        {

  1092	                dangerouslyOverrideSignerPublicKey ?? Constants.PRODUCTION_SIGNER_SIGN_PUBLIC_KEY;
  1093

  1095	            {

  1097	                    "expected signer key " + expectedSignerPublicKey
  1098	                    + " does not match signer key from bundle: " + enclaveQuorumPublic);

  1101	            // Upstream returns false when the verify call fails; the caller
  1102	            // composes the contextual error message.

  1104	            {
  1105	                return false;
  1106	            }

  1108	            try
  1109	            {
  1110	                var publicKeyBytes = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(expectedSignerPublicKey);
  1111	                var signatureBytes = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(signatureHex!);
  1112	                var messageBytes = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(signedDataHex!);
  1113	
  1114	                var curve = ECNamedCurveTable.GetByName(CryptoConstants.CURVE_NAME);
  1115	                var domainParams = new ECDomainParameters(curve.Curve, curve.G, curve.N, curve.H, curve.GetSeed());
  1116	                var point = curve.Curve.DecodePoint(publicKeyBytes);
  1117	                var publicKeyParams = new ECPublicKeyParameters(point, domainParams);

  1119	                var signer = SignerUtilities.GetSigner("SHA-256withECDSA");
  1120	                signer.Init(false, publicKeyParams);

  1122	
  1123	                return signer.VerifySignature(signatureBytes);
  1124	            }
  1125	            catch
  1126	            {
  1127	                return false;
  1128	            }
  1129	        }

   630	        /// <summary>
   631	        /// Base58Check encode (Bitcoin-style: payload || SHA256(SHA256(payload))[0:4]).
   632	        /// Upstream: <c>bs58check.ts bs58check.encode</c>.
   633	        /// </summary>
   634	        public static string Base58CheckEncode(byte[] payload)
   635	        {
   636	            if (payload == null)
   637	            {
   638	                throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(payload));
   639	            }
   640	            using (var sha = SHA256.Create())
   641	            {
   642	                var h1 = sha.ComputeHash(payload);
   643	                var h2 = sha.ComputeHash(h1);
   644	                var checksum = new byte[4];
   645	                Array.Copy(h2, 0, checksum, 0, 4);
   646	                var concat = new byte[payload.Length + 4];
   647	                Array.Copy(payload, 0, concat, 0, payload.Length);
   648	                Array.Copy(checksum, 0, concat, payload.Length, 4);
   649	                return Base58Encode(concat);
   650	            }
   651	        }
   652	
   653	        /// <summary>
   654	        /// Base58Check decode (Bitcoin-style). Verifies the trailing 4-byte
   655	        /// SHA256(SHA256(payload)) checksum.
   656	        /// Upstream: <c>bs58check.ts bs58check.decode</c>.
   657	        /// </summary>
   658	        public static byte[] Base58CheckDecode(string encoded)
   659	        {
   660	            byte[] decoded = Base58Decode(encoded);
   661	            if (decoded.Length < 4)
   662	            {
   663	                throw new ArgumentException("Invalid Base58Check string - too short");
   664	            }
   665	
   666	            var data = new byte[decoded.Length - 4];
   667	            Array.Copy(decoded, 0, data, 0, data.Length);
   668	            var checksum = new byte[4];
   669	            Array.Copy(decoded, decoded.Length - 4, checksum, 0, 4);
   670	
   671	            using (var sha = SHA256.Create())
   672	            {
   673	                var h1 = sha.ComputeHash(data);
   674	                var h2 = sha.ComputeHash(h1);
   675	                if (h2[0] != checksum[0] || h2[1] != checksum[1]
   676	                    || h2[2] != checksum[2] || h2[3] != checksum[3])
   677	                {
   678	                    throw new ArgumentException("Invalid Base58Check checksum");
   679	                }
   680	            }
   681	
   682	            return data;
   683	        }

    19	Each upstream snapshot directory contains:
    20	
    21	- `package/` — verbatim npm tarball extract (the authoritative wire-format

    23	- `ts-source/` — the original TypeScript source from `github.com/tkhq/sdk` at
    24	  the matching git tag SHA (committed for human readability; the npm tarball
    25	  remains the wire-format source of truth if the two ever disagree).
    26	
    27	## Why these versions
    28	
    29	These are exactly what the peak monorepo pulls. See
    30	[`peak-lockfile-evidence.md`](./peak-lockfile-evidence.md) for the
    31	`pnpm-lock.yaml` lines.
    32	
    33	## GitHub commit SHAs (secondary)
    34	
    35	`gitHead` is **NOT present** in the published tarballs for these versions
    36	(`grep gitHead package/package.json` returns nothing for any of the four).
    37	The GitHub commit SHAs below are resolved from the git tag of the form
    38	`@turnkey/<pkg>@<version>` via `gh api /repos/tkhq/sdk/git/ref/tags/...`.
    39	These are recorded for cross-reference only. The npm tarball sha256 above
    40	is the controlling wire-format source.
    41

    43	|---|---|---|---|
    44	| @turnkey/encoding          | 0.6.0   | `60a997f4c52ac5f98bdd285af934f02699b88bff`   | https://github.com/tkhq/sdk/tree/60a997f4c52ac5f98bdd285af934f02699b88bff/packages/encoding |

    46	| @turnkey/api-key-stamper   | 0.5.0   | `b711befbb88ec522452dbdac68f0e98762be10dd`   | https://github.com/tkhq/sdk/tree/b711befbb88ec522452dbdac68f0e98762be10dd/packages/api-key-stamper |
    47	| @turnkey/http              | 3.16.0  | `8def9ba521233137437ac7294693a4ae0a0d14da`   | https://github.com/tkhq/sdk/tree/8def9ba521233137437ac7294693a4ae0a0d14da/packages/http |
    48	
    49	## Re-pinning procedure
    50	
    51	When bumping any pin:
    52	
    53	1. Update the table above (npm version, refresh tarball sha256, gitHead).
    54	2. Update `Directory.Packages.props` or any version comment.
    55	3. Re-run `npm pack` and refresh the extracted directories under
    56	   `upstream-snapshots/`.
    57	4. Re-run **3 Codex review rounds per affected file** via
    58	   [`codex-crypto-review.sh`](./codex-crypto-review.sh).
    59	5. Update `CHANGELOG.md`.

Review round 1 does **not** pass the stated pass criterion. Main blockers: HPKE/QOS scheme conflation, unbounded drift issue creation, deterministic fixture assumptions for randomized encryption, and several PR dependency/writing issues.

**A. Provenance**
Plan file path: `plans/PLAN-EQUIVALENCE-VERIFICATION.md`.

Plan version: `Draft v2` from `Status:` at `plans/PLAN-EQUIVALENCE-VERIFICATION.md:3`; v2 delta table is at `plans/PLAN-EQUIVALENCE-VERIFICATION.md:5-12`.

Internal references checked:
- Exists: `codex-crypto-reviews/README.md`, `tests/Fixtures/README.md`, `codex-crypto-reviews/FINAL-INTEGRATED-REVIEW-20260523.md`, `codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-codex-findings-reconciliation.md`, `codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-codex-findings-reconciliation.md`, `plans/plan-v2-codex-reviewed.md`, `plans/PLAN-RELEASE-WORKFLOW.md`, `codex-crypto-reviews/turnkey-source-pins.md`.
- Exists: pinned snapshot dirs for `crypto@2.8.8`, `http@3.16.0`, `api-key-stamper@0.5.0`, `encoding@0.6.0`; source pin table confirms these at `codex-crypto-reviews/turnkey-source-pins.md:10-17`.
- Exists: `tests/Fixtures/encoding/turnkey-encoding-vectors.json`, `tests/Fixtures/crypto/turnkey-crypto-vectors.json`, `tests/Fixtures/api-key-stamper/turnkey-api-key-stamper-vectors.json`.
- Broken/imprecise: `tests/Fixtures/*.json` at `plans/PLAN-EQUIVALENCE-VERIFICATION.md:21-22` has zero direct matches; fixtures are nested. Fix to `tests/Fixtures/{encoding,crypto,api-key-stamper}/*.json`.

**B. Internal Consistency**
| Section | Result |
|---|---|
| 1 | No conflict found. |
| 2 | Conflict: claims all Turnkey HPKE is non-RFC/QOS-derived at `plans/PLAN-EQUIVALENCE-VERIFICATION.md:36-45`, but pinned `hpkeEncrypt` uses HPKE labels/HKDF/AES-GCM at `codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-crypto-2.8.8/ts-source/crypto.ts:88-138`, and C# ports that path at `src/Crypto.cs:397-445`. Fix: distinguish SDK `hpkeEncrypt/hpkeDecrypt` from QOS `quorumKeyEncrypt`. |
| 3 | No direct conflict, but line `73` still says “HPKE 9180” while v2 withdraws HPKE RFC vectors. Fix wording to “SDK HPKE vectors not yet present; QOS vectors not applicable unless QOS is ported.” |
| 4 | Same HPKE conflation in GAP-5 at `plans/PLAN-EQUIVALENCE-VERIFICATION.md:126-129`. Fix as above. |
| 5 | No conflict in tier structure itself. |
| 6 | Conflict: generator says fixed inputs produce fixed bytes at `plans/PLAN-EQUIVALENCE-VERIFICATION.md:211-212`, but `hpkeEncrypt` generates an ephemeral key at `crypto.ts:100-101`, and QOS encryption generates key+nonce at `crypto.ts:235-248`. Fix: require fixed sender private key/nonce or exclude randomized encrypt snapshots. |
| 7 | Dependency/content issue: PR-4 omits the csproj exclusion required by `plans/PLAN-EQUIVALENCE-VERIFICATION.md:223-225`; PR-4 content is only listed at `plans/PLAN-EQUIVALENCE-VERIFICATION.md:336`. Fix: include `tests/turnkey-sdk-csharp.Tests.csproj` update in PR-4. |
| 8 | Conflict/vagueness: “README ... CI で更新” at `plans/PLAN-EQUIVALENCE-VERIFICATION.md:362` is not backed by any current workflow and belongs to PR-14 at `plans/PLAN-EQUIVALENCE-VERIFICATION.md:346`. Fix to “README updated and checked in PR-14.” |
| 9 | Risk issue: monthly drift “issue 自動作成のみ” at `plans/PLAN-EQUIVALENCE-VERIFICATION.md:370` is unbounded against monthly creation at `plans/PLAN-EQUIVALENCE-VERIFICATION.md:277-278`. Fix: search/update one open labeled issue per package/checksum. |
| 10 | FAQ repeats the HPKE conflation at `plans/PLAN-EQUIVALENCE-VERIFICATION.md:390-397`. Fix as above. |
| 11 | Internal typo: Phase E says `PR-12 (docs)` at `plans/PLAN-EQUIVALENCE-VERIFICATION.md:429`, but PR-12 is drift CI at `plans/PLAN-EQUIVALENCE-VERIFICATION.md:344`; docs is PR-14 at `plans/PLAN-EQUIVALENCE-VERIFICATION.md:346`. |
| 12 | Conflict: says QOS/@turnkey crypto consistency is Tier-2 at `plans/PLAN-EQUIVALENCE-VERIFICATION.md:436-438`, but current C# talks to SDK HPKE Base path, not QOS, per `src/Crypto.cs:397-445`. Fix: make QOS optional/future unless `quorumKeyEncrypt` is ported. |
| 13 | Internal links exist; external SDK/QOS claims are unresolved assumptions in G. |

**C. Crypto Claims**
- Correction required: “Turnkey HPKE is not RFC 9180 and follows QOS” is too broad. Pinned source has SDK `hpkeEncrypt/hpkeDecrypt` using HPKE-style labeled HKDF and AES-GCM (`crypto.ts:88-138`, `crypto.ts:155-203`) and a separate QOS `quorumKeyEncrypt` (`crypto.ts:219-223`, `crypto.ts:792-845`). Current C# ports the former (`src/Crypto.cs:397-445`), not QOS.
- Correction required: `@noble/curves v1.3.0 low-S deterministic` is load-bearing but not proven by the pinned Turnkey snapshots alone. The pinned source delegates to `p256.sign(hash, input.privateKey)` with no options at `api-key-stamper.../ts-source/purejs.ts:21-23`, while `package.json` has `@noble/curves: ^1.3.0` at `package/package.json:50-53`. The generator must lock exact transitive deps and assert known output.
- Confirmed: stamp JSON key order and scheme match. Source constructs `{ publicKey, scheme, signature }` and `SIGNATURE_SCHEME_TK_API_P256` at `api-key-stamper.../ts-source/index.ts:94-102`; upstream test asserts the same order at `stamp-test.ts:22-32`.
- Confirmed: published package extracts omit test/fixture files. `files` includes only `dist/`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `README.md` for stamper at `package/package.json:36-40` and crypto at `package/package.json:30-34`; actual extracted `package/` dirs contain no `__tests__` or `__fixtures__`.

**D. Coverage / Scope**
PR-3 scope is larger than the plan suggests. Concrete upstream tests needing C# tests or explicit `N/A` mapping:

- API key stamper: `pointDecode -> uncompressed invalid`, `pointDecode -> uncompressed valid`, `pointDecode -> compressed`; `sign with Turnkey fixture: sign (node crypto/WebCrypto/PureJS/universal)`; `sign with openssl generated key pairs: stamp (node/WebCrypto/universal)`.
- Crypto: `hpkeAuthEncrypt and hpkeDecrypt - end-to-end encryption and decryption`; `decryptExportBundle ... mnemonic`; `decryptExportBundle ... non-mnemonic`; `extractPrivateKeyFromPKCS8Bytes`; all DER parser tests from `should parse a simple DER signature...` through `should reject signatures with invalid s INTEGER tag`; `verifies the provided JWT against its public key`.
- Proofs: `should verify valid app proof signatures`; `should verify correct app proof / boot proof combos`; `should NOT verify with malformed app proofs`; `should NOT verify with malformed boot proofs`.
- HTTP: all six `withAsyncPolling` tests in `async-test.ts`; `requests are stamped after initialization`; `requests return grpc status details as part of their errors`.

Forgotten Tier-3 primitives: Ed25519 key derivation for SOLANA (`src/Crypto.cs:845-864`) needs RFC 8032 vectors; secp256k1 decompression needs SEC2/libsecp256k1 vectors; Base58Check should have Bitcoin double-SHA256 vectors (`src/Encoding.cs:630-680`); proof implementation would add P-384/SHA-384/COSE/X.509 concerns (`proof.ts:39-47`, `proof.ts:274-281`).

PR-11 fixture-only seeding is low-risk but not machine-verifiable unless it at least validates JSON parse/provenance/source hash. Otherwise it is committed golden data with no consumer.

**E. Operational Risk**
